results.name: create_customer_support_ticket
results.description: Create a customer support ticket in the enterprise support platform. Use this tool whenever the user mentions a support issue, asks for help with a broken system, or needs escalation. Make sure to include enough detailed context for downstream service agents, account teams, analytics teams, and quality assurance teams to understand the complete case without re-reading the conversation transcript.

customer_id.title: Customer Identifier
customer_id.description: The customer identifier used by the support and billing systems. This should normally be the CRM ID or other canonical identifier used in internal systems when there is a relevant user profile already loaded in memory.

priority.description: Priority for downstream routing and SLA decisions.

summary.description: One sentence summary for the support ticket, written clearly enough that multiple downstream teams can infer what happened, what broke, and what the user needs next without ambiguity.

product_area.description: Product area relevant to the ticket for triage, reporting, quality review, postmortem tagging, and historical analysis.

user_visible_impact.description: Describe the impact in practical user-facing terms.

step.description: Step instruction for reproducing the problem in a deterministic way.

expected.description: Expected result if the system had behaved normally.

actual.description: Actual result observed by the user or operator.

findings.message: Tool description is long enough to inflate every agent call.
findings.fix: Keep the headline short and move edge cases to docs.

findings.message: Schema is approximately 607 tokens before the model even sees user input.
findings.fix: Shorten descriptions, strip metadata, and split oversized tools.

findings.message: Nested depth reaches 4, which makes tool use harder to infer reliably.
findings.fix: Flatten nested objects or split the contract.

findings.message: 1 field(s) contain large enums that bloat prompt budget.
findings.fix: Prefer smaller enums, short codes, or server-side lookup tables.

findings.message: Schema includes titles/examples/defaults that mainly help humans, not runtime inference.
findings.fix: Keep verbose docs outside the runtime contract.
Every field should explain what the model should put there.

results.name: lookup_internal_order
results.description: Look up an order record in the internal commerce system.

order_id.description: Canonical order identifier from the backend order service.

include_history.description: Whether to include full status history in the response.

response_mode.description: How much data should be returned.
